title: 7 Herbal Hangover Tinctures to Try This Weekend

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hangover because it has strong antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the body from damage caused by excessive drinking.

Bilobalide is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation, relaxes the mind, and replenishes the body's antioxidants, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and irritability.

Isorhapontigenin is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are often caused by excessive drinking and can lead to hangover symptoms.
Flavonoids are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which are often caused by excessive drinking and can lead to hangover symptoms.

Kaempferol is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate headaches and other hangover symptoms caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is sometimes used to help with hangover symptoms. This is because ginkgo biloba has properties that may help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow in the brain. When we drink too much alcohol, it can cause inflammation and reduce blood flow to the brain, leading to headaches and other symptoms. The antioxidants in ginkgo biloba may help reduce this inflammation and improve blood flow, which could help alleviate some of the hangover symptoms. Additionally, ginkgo biloba is believed to have a protective effect on the brain and may help reduce the damage caused by excessive drinking. Some people also use it to help with nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of a hangover. The tincture can be taken in small amounts, often mixed with water or another liquid, to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tinctures that help with hangover, start by gathering the required ingredients. You will need Withania somnifera roots or dried leaves, 80-100 proof vodka or a similar high-proof alcohol, and a clean glass jar with a lid. Begin by measuring out the Withania somnifera material and filling the jar to about two-thirds capacity. Pour the vodka over the material, making sure that the Withania somnifera is completely covered by the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and label it for future reference. Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the tincture extract. After the sitting period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This tincture can be stored in a cool, dark place and taken as needed to alleviate hangover symptoms. The recommended dosage is a few drops of the tincture, mixed with water, taken before or after a night of drinking.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for hangover because they help reduce nausea, alleviate inflammation, and improve digestion, making them an effective natural remedy to alleviate hangover symptoms.

Shogaols are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and pain in the body, alleviate nausea and vomiting, and also have antioxidant properties that can help protect the liver from damage caused by excessive drinking.

Zingiberene is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation, prevents oxidative stress, and improves liver function, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and nausea.
Volatile oils, also known as ashwagandha, contain compounds that may help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and stabilizing blood sugar levels.

Curcuminoids are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by protecting the body from damage caused by excessive drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help alleviate hangover symptoms. The tincture is made from the root of the ginger plant and contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols. When taken, these compounds can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea, which is a common symptom of hangovers. Ginger also has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help ease headaches and body aches. Additionally, ginger can help stimulate digestion and improve blood flow, making it easier to recover from a night of drinking. The tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water, making it a convenient option for those looking for a natural hangover remedy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera tincture to help with hangovers, you will need dried Withania somnifera roots, a clean glass jar, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, dry the Withania somnifera roots thoroughly in a warm place or using a dehydrator. Once dry, chop the roots into small pieces and place them in the glass jar. Then, pour the carrier oil over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If you prefer a tincture that doesn't contain alcohol, you can use glycerin instead of vodka. To do this, simply combine the infused oil with the glycerin in a 1:1 ratio. Finally, transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take 20-30 drops as needed to help alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation in the body and prevent damage to cells, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Polyphenols help to reduce hangover symptoms because they have antioxidant properties that can neutralize free radicals in the body, reduce inflammation, and protect the liver from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Tannins help to cure hangover because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce nausea, vomiting, and headaches caused by excessive drinking.
Quercetin is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce headaches and nausea associated with hangover symptoms.
Saponins are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation, protect liver cells, and have antioxidant properties that counteract the damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with hangovers. The tincture is made from the plant's flowers and leaves. It contains chemicals that can calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms after a night of drinking too much. When taken in small amounts, it can help to regulate the body's response to stress and promote relaxation. This can lead to a decrease in nausea and vomiting, which are often uncomfortable side effects of hangovers. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata has been shown to have a sedative effect, helping to regulate sleep patterns and promote a restful night's sleep, which is essential for recovery. By using the tincture, some people may be able to reduce their hangover symptoms and feel better faster. The tincture is often taken in small amounts, usually a few drops, mixed with water, and taken at the first sign of hangover symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tincture, start by gathering high-quality dried Withania somnifera roots. You will need 250 grams of roots for a standard batch. Combine the dried roots with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka, like 80% or 100% vodka, in a clean glass jar. Make sure the roots are completely covered by the vodka. Close the jar tightly and place it in a dark, cool place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the active compounds from the roots into the vodka. After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This liquid is your Withania somnifera tincture, which you can take in small amounts to help alleviate hangover symptoms. It's recommended to start with a small dose of 10-20 drops and adjust according to your needs.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea, vomiting, and headaches caused by excessive drinking, and also has antioxidant properties that can help repair damaged cells in the body.
Valeranone is good for hangover because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce headache, nausea, and fatigue caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Valerenal is good for hangover because it reduces inflammation and pain, and also helps to prevent nausea and vomiting, making it easier to recover from a night of heavy drinking.
Isovaleric acid helps alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing inflammation, soothing the stomach, and rehydrating the body due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture, also known as valerian root tincture, can be a helpful remedy for hangover symptoms. This is because valerian root contains compounds that have a sedative effect on the body, which can help calm the stomach and ease nausea. Many people experience stomach discomfort and vomiting after a night of drinking, and valerian root may help reduce these symptoms. Additionally, valerian root has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate headaches and fatigue that often accompany hangovers. When taken as a tincture, valerian root can be absorbed quickly into the body, providing fast relief from hangover symptoms. Some people also find that valerian root tincture helps promote relaxation and reduces anxiety, which can be beneficial when trying to recover from a night of overindulgence.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tincture that can help with hangovers, you will need dried Withania somnifera roots, vodka, and a glass jar. First, fill the jar with 1 part of dried Withania somnifera roots and add 2 parts of vodka, making sure the roots are completely covered. Then, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active ingredients into the vodka. After the 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another jar, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the tincture to smaller dropper bottles and store them in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture, take 20-30 drops of it in water or another liquid when you feel a hangover coming on or after a night of drinking. Withania somnifera is known for its adaptogenic properties, which can help your body recover from stress and hangovers by reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinic acid may help alleviate hangover symptoms due to its ability to protect the liver from oxidative stress caused by excessive alcohol consumption, reducing the severity of hangover-related inflammation and discomfort.
Quinine is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and nausea in the body caused by excessive drinking, and also has antioxidant properties that aid in detoxifying the body.
Quinidine is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue caused by excessive drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tincture is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. It contains a compound called quinine, which is known to help alleviate symptoms of hangovers. When you drink too much alcohol, it can cause dehydration, headaches, and nausea. Cinchona officinalis tincture helps to combat these symptoms in several ways. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the pain and swelling in the head and body. It also has a mild diuretic effect, which can help to replenish lost fluids and electrolytes. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tincture can help to stabilize blood sugar levels, which can become imbalanced due to excessive drinking. This can lead to feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness, which quinine can help to alleviate. As a result, taking Cinchona officinalis tincture can provide relief from hangover symptoms, allowing you to feel better and recover faster.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tinctures that help with hangover, start by harvesting the roots of the Withania somnifera plant, also known as ashwagandha. You can purchase dried roots from a health store or grow the plant yourself. Next, chop the roots into small pieces and mix them with a solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is 1 part Withania somnifera roots to 2 parts solvent. Close the jar and store it in a dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots release their active properties into the solvent. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the medicinal compounds from the plant. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Withania somnifera tincture is now ready to use, and you can take a few drops of it to help alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea, headaches, and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ginsenosides are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea by reducing inflammation in the brain and digestive system.

Polyphenols are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation, protect the liver from damage, and have antioxidant properties that can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headache and fatigue.

Saponins are good for hangover because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can be triggered by excessive drinking and contribute to hangover symptoms.

Flavonoids are good for hangover because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Triterpenoids are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and alleviate oxidative stress in the body, making them a natural remedy to soothe hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tincture is a natural remedy that may help alleviate hangover symptoms. It contains compounds that can help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to hangover discomfort. Ginsenosides, the active ingredients in ginseng, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce nausea and fatigue. Additionally, ginseng may help improve mental clarity and reduce stress, which can be beneficial in helping to recover from a night of heavy drinking. Ginseng tincture can also help to replenish energy levels and support the body's natural detoxification process, making it a potential aid in recovering from a hangover.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal tincture that helps with hangover, you will need dried Withania somnifera roots, a clean glass jar, a clean glass bottle, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, fill the glass jar with the dried roots, making sure to pack them tightly. Next, pour the solvent over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part roots to 2 parts solvent. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the roots infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. You now have a tincture that can be taken as needed to alleviate hangover symptoms. It's recommended to take 10-20 drops of the tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce hangover symptoms like nausea, fatigue, and headaches.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal tinctures for hangover

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Withanolides are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms, such as headaches and nausea, by soothing the digestive system and calming the mind.

Flavonoids help reduce hangover symptoms because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate headaches, soothe stomach issues, and improve overall recovery after a night of heavy drinking.

Saponins are good for hangover because they help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which are often caused by excessive drinking and can lead to hangover symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.
Alkaloids, also known as Ashwagandha, are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and headaches caused by excessive alcohol consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as Ashwagandha, is a plant used in traditional medicine to help with hangover symptoms. The tincture is made from the plant's roots and is known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the head and body. This can be especially helpful for people who experience migraines or headaches after drinking. The tincture is also believed to help with stress and anxiety, which are common causes of hangover symptoms. By reducing stress and anxiety, the tincture can help regulate the body's natural response to a hangover, making it easier to recover. Additionally, Withania somnifera has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, a common result of excessive drinking.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera tincture that helps with hangovers, start by gathering high-quality Withania somnifera roots and stems. Next, chop these roots into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar. Add a neutral solvent, such as 80% to 100% ethanol or 80% glycerin, to the jar, making sure the plant material is completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part plant material to 2 parts solvent. Secure the jar with a lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 to 4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts to help alleviate hangover symptoms, such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal tinctures for hangover

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can help alleviate hangover symptoms. For example, Ginkgo biloba is known for its ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can help ease headaches and other hangover-related pain. It also contains antioxidants that protect the body from damage caused by excessive drinking.  Zingiber officinale, or ginger, is another popular herb used in hangover tinctures. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, common symptoms of hangovers. Ginger also has natural anti-emetic properties, which can help stabilize the digestive system and reduce the risk of vomiting.  Fixing hangover issues can improve life in several ways. It can help reduce the risk of dehydration, which can lead to more serious health problems. Additionally, hangovers can disrupt daily routines and affect productivity, making it harder to tackle tasks and responsibilities. By alleviating hangover symptoms, individuals can regain control over their daily lives and maintain a better overall sense of well-being.  Herbal tinctures can be a safe and natural alternative to traditional hangover remedies, such as painkillers and antacids. They can be taken in moderation and in combination with other herbs to create a customized blend that meets individual needs. To use herbal tinctures effectively, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional or a qualified herbalist to determine the best course of treatment.
supplementary_best: For hangover remedy, combine Ginkgo biloba, Withania somnifera, and Panax ginseng tinctures. Ginkgo biloba helps with blood flow and oxygen delivery to the brain, which can alleviate headaches. Withania somnifera, also known as Ashwagandha, reduces inflammation and stress response, which can contribute to hangover symptoms. Panax ginseng, an adaptogenic herb, helps the body adapt to stress and promotes balance in the body's energy and vitality. This combination addresses the physical and mental symptoms of hangover, providing relief from headaches, fatigue, and stress.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a hangover, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. Tinctures made from herbs like 'Kola nut' and 'Yohimbe' can be bad for you when you're hungover. Kola nut can make your heart beat faster and raise your blood pressure, which can be a problem if you're already dehydrated from drinking too much. Yohimbe can also increase your heart rate and blood pressure, and it can interact with other medications you might be taking to help with your hangover.  Tinctures made from 'Ephedra' can also be a problem when you're hungover. Ephedra can increase your heart rate and blood pressure, which can be a serious issue if you're already dehydrated. It can also interact with other medications, including some pain relievers and blood thinners.  Another herb to avoid is 'Cassia'. Cassia can cause stomach problems and interact with other medications, including blood thinners. If you're hungover, you might already be feeling nauseous and experiencing stomach problems, so taking a cassia tincture could make things worse.  Lastly, 'Bladderwrack' tinctures can be a problem when you're hungover. Bladderwrack can cause stomach problems and interact with other medications, including blood thinners. It can also lower your blood pressure, which can be a problem if you're already feeling lightheaded from dehydration.
supplementary_other_remedies: When you wake up after a night of drinking, a hangover can be a real pain. Besides herbal tinctures, there are some other natural remedies that can help. Drinking plenty of water can rehydrate your body and help flush out the toxins from the night before. This is especially important if you're feeling dehydrated or experiencing headaches. Coconut water is a good option as it contains electrolytes that can help rebalance your body's fluids.  Another remedy is to take a warm bath or shower. This can help relax your muscles and ease any tension or discomfort. The heat can also help open up your pores and promote sweating, which can help remove some of the toxins from your body. Some people also swear by exercise, like going for a gentle jog or doing some yoga. While it might not sound appealing when you're feeling hungover, exercise can actually help stimulate blood flow and oxygenation to your brain, making you feel more alert and awake.  Ginger is another natural remedy that can help alleviate hangover symptoms. You can drink ginger tea, eat ginger in its raw form, or even try ginger ale. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe your stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, some people find that eating a small amount of food, like toast or crackers, can help settle their stomach and prevent further nausea. This is because food can help slow down the digestion of any remaining alcohol in your system, making you feel less queasy.  Lastly, some people find that getting some fresh air and taking a short walk outside can be beneficial. Being in nature and getting some sunlight can help regulate your body's natural rhythms and improve your mood. This can be especially helpful if you're feeling groggy or disoriented after a night of drinking.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people have a hangover, they often feel very sick. They might have headaches, feel tired and weak, and have a dry, sore throat. Many people with hangovers also experience stomach problems like nausea and vomiting, as their body tries to get rid of the leftover alcohol. Medicinal herbs can help with some of these symptoms.  Ginger, for example, is good for nausea. It has natural chemicals that can calm the stomach and reduce vomiting. You can eat ginger in the form of tea, candies, or even raw ginger.  Willow bark contains salicin, a chemical similar to aspirin. It can help with headaches by reducing swelling in the blood vessels of the brain.  Peppermint tea can ease a sore throat and help with nausea. The menthol in peppermint can also help calm a queasy stomach.  Licorice root can help with stomach problems like nausea and vomiting. It has chemicals that can protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.  Milk thistle is a herb that can help with liver problems. The liver is the part of the body that breaks down alcohol, so if it gets damaged, you can feel sick for a long time. Milk thistle can help repair liver cells and get the liver working properly again.  These medicinal herbs can be used to help people with hangover symptoms. They can be taken in the form of tea, capsules, or even raw.
